From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Russian pair skater (born 1989)

For the ice dancer of the same name, see [Arina Ushakova (ice dancer)](/wiki/Arina_Ushakova_(ice_dancer) "Arina Ushakova (ice dancer)").

Arina Ushakova

| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | [Figure skating](/wiki/Figure_skating "Figure skating"): [Pairs](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating") | | | | Representing  [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") (with Karev) | | | | [Winter Universiade](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_Winter_Universiade "Figure skating at the Winter Universiade") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2007 Turin](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_2007_Winter_Universiade "Figure skating at the 2007 Winter Universiade") | Pairs | | Representing  [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") (with Popov) | | | | [European Youth Olympic Festival](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_European_Youth_Olympic_Festival "Figure skating at the European Youth Olympic Festival") | | | | Gold medal – first place | [2003 Bled](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_2003_European_Youth_Olympic_Festival "Figure skating at the 2003 European Youth Olympic Festival") | Pairs | | |

**Arina Andreyevna Ushakova** ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Арина Андреевна Ушакова; born 18 December 1989) is a Russian [pair skating](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating") coach and former competitor. With [Sergei Karev](/wiki/Sergei_Karev "Sergei Karev"), she is the [2005 Golden Spin of Zagreb](/wiki/2005_Golden_Spin_of_Zagreb "2005 Golden Spin of Zagreb") silver medalist and [2007 Winter Universiade](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_2007_Winter_Universiade "Figure skating at the 2007 Winter Universiade") bronze medalist. They placed 5th at the [2008 European Championships](/wiki/2008_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2008 European Figure Skating Championships").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arina_Ushakova_(pair_skater)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Ushakova was born 18 December 1989 in [Perm](/wiki/Perm,_Russia "Perm, Russia").[[1]](#cite_note-ISU-0708-1) She is married to former pair skater [Vladislav Zhovnirski](/wiki/Vladislav_Zhovnirski "Vladislav Zhovnirski"), with whom she has a child (born c. 2016).[[2]](#cite_note-FSFR160812-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arina_Ushakova_(pair_skater)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Ushakova teamed up with [Alexander Popov](/wiki/Alexander_Popov_(figure_skater) "Alexander Popov (figure skater)") in 2000. After training under [Liudmila Kalinina](/wiki/Liudmila_Kalinina "Liudmila Kalinina"), they moved to the husband-and-wife team of Valentina and Valeri Tiukov, who coached them in [Perm](/wiki/Perm,_Russia "Perm, Russia").[[3]](#cite_note-ISU-0405-3) Ushakova/Popov won four medals on the [JGP](/wiki/ISU_Junior_Grand_Prix "ISU Junior Grand Prix") series – one gold, two silver, and one bronze – and qualified twice for the [JGP Final](/wiki/ISU_Junior_Grand_Prix_Final "ISU Junior Grand Prix Final"). Their partnership ended in 2005.

Ushakova teamed up with Sergei Karev in 2005, moving from Perm to Moscow in order to train with him.[[1]](#cite_note-ISU-0708-1) Originally coached by [Nina Mozer](/wiki/Nina_Mozer "Nina Mozer"), the pair switched to [Natalia Pavlova](/wiki/Natalia_Pavlova "Natalia Pavlova") ahead of the 2007–08 season.[[1]](#cite_note-ISU-0708-1) Ushakova/Karev won the bronze medal at the [2008 Russian Nationals](/wiki/2008_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "2008 Russian Figure Skating Championships") and were sent to the [2008 European Championships](/wiki/2008_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2008 European Figure Skating Championships") where they finished 5th. In the long program, they had a fall on a lift but were not injured.[[4]](#cite_note-ASI080124-4) They split at the end of the season due to Karev's serious illness.[[5]](#cite_note-Sovsp081025-5) After a tryout with [Jerome Blanchard](/wiki/Jerome_Blanchard "Jerome Blanchard"),[[5]](#cite_note-Sovsp081025-5) she decided to retire from competition.

Ushakova works as a coach in Moscow. She has coached Arina Cherniavskaia / [Antonio Souza-Kordeyru](/wiki/Antonio_Souza-Kordeyru "Antonio Souza-Kordeyru")[[6]](#cite_note-ISU-ACAS-6) and Albina Sokur / Roman Pleshkov.

International Skating Union. Archived from [the original](http://figureskating.sportresult.com/Bios/RUS/2016/0/51700/MEN/TO/6267) on 12 August 2016.
